title: Nomination and Implementation Support
focus: Outlines the procedure for nominating documentary heritage and the roles of the UNESCO Secretariat and National Committees.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 010]
Nomination Procedure
Any organization or individual can nominate a documentary item.
Nominations are submitted via UNESCO Member States through their National Commission for UNESCO (or relevant government body).
* Two proposals per UNESCO Member State per nomination cycle.
* Unlimited joint nomination proposals from two or more Member States.

[Page 011]
Support & Implementation
* The Secretariat at UNESCO: Provides support to the IAC, general administration, and program monitoring. Serves as the primary contact point.
* Regional and National Committees: Implement the program on the ground. These committees are autonomous from UNESCO and consist of dedicated local heritage professionals.
